nodes.name: BuildHMCKernelFromLogDensityOracle
nodes.description: Creates a pure Hamiltonian Monte Carlo transition kernel from a provided target log-density oracle, with stochasticity and chain state threaded explicitly.

inputs.name: target_log_kernel
inputs.constraints: Stateless oracle; no persistent writes; deterministic for fixed input.

outputs.name: hmc_transition_kernel
outputs.constraints: Pure transition; consumes and returns new PRNGKey; HMCState is immutable state_in->state_out and may include position, momentum, mass_matrix, and trace diagnostics.

nodes.conceptual_summary: Pure Symplectic Markov Transition Constructor from Scalar Potential Oracle. Transforms a deterministic oracle over a continuous state vector into a stochastic, state-to-state transition operator by introducing auxiliary momentum variables and numerically integrating coupled position-momentum dynamics. Each proposal is generated with a volume-preserving, approximately energy-conserving flow, then accept/reject corrected to target the desired invariant distribution. Randomness and chain state are threaded explicitly, yielding a referentially transparent transition function.. Applications: Sampling high-dimensional molecular conformation spaces under differentiable energy models in computational chemistry, Posterior exploration of latent parameter fields in geophysical inverse problems with differentiable forward models, Uncertainty-aware calibration of nonlinear dynamical system parameters in aerospace guidance and control, Bayesian reconstruction of spatially varying material properties in computational mechanics
